mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market signals an impending economic contraction, causing investors to downgrade earnings expectations and sell equities, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the rising unemployment signal, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns exceeding 5% within the horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising mortgage debt outstanding and falling housing starts indicates credit-driven demand exhaustion, triggering a broad-based pullback in consumer spending and corporate earnings that depresses S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no predictive power for quarterly returns, or SPX forward returns remain positive when mortgage debt growth exceeds housing starts growth by more than 2 standard deviations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during early recovery phases reflects expanding capacity utilization and improving corporate margins, which drives positive earnings revisions and lifts S&P 500 valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth decouples from equity returns, or SPX forward returns are negative during consecutive quarters of rising INDPRO above 105.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation forces market pricing of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, compressing equity valuation multiples and triggering risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 returns within a week.
failure_condition: Equity multiples expand despite inflation exceeding the 0.4% monthly threshold, or 5-day forward returns show no significant negative skew during high-inflation months.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly accelerations in real GDP growth reflect expanding aggregate demand and corporate cash flows, which mechanically support higher equity valuations and produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth accelerations consistently fail to predict positive quarterly returns, or the signal produces negative forward returns in > 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in total nonfarm employment and declining unemployment confirms broad-based economic resilience, reducing recession risk premiums and supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform single-series baselines, or SPX forward returns are negative when PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls simultaneously over a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts during a credit contraction signals an immediate demand shock to the broader economy, triggering short-term equity risk-off positioning and driving negative S&P 500 returns within a week.
failure_condition: Housing starts decline below the threshold without corresponding equity drawdowns, or 5-day forward returns remain positive during consecutive months of sub-1300 housing starts.
